sanskrit:
त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः।
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत्॥
transliteration:
tri-vidhaṃ narakasyedaṃ dvāraṃ nāśanam ātmanaḥ |
kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṃ tyajet ||
literal_meaning: Three-fold / of hell / this / gate / destructive / of the self / lust / anger / and also / greed / therefore / these / three / should abandon
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns that lust, anger, and greed are the three psychological gateways to self-destruction; consciously renouncing them prevents inner hell-states and preserves the authentic self.
psychological_state: Heightened vigilance and determination to identify and release the three core addictive drives before they crystallize into destructive action.
trigger_scenario: Feeling the surge of desire when scrolling social media, the spike of anger in traffic, or the clutch of greed during a flash sale—recognizing these as portals to an inner hell of rumination and regret.
anchor_text: Drop lust, anger, and greed to keep your true self intact.
